_comment: Cross-binding conformance reference for interp.searchsorted (esm-spec §9.2). Index outputs are exact (no rounding tolerance). Each `success` scenario gives positional inputs [x, xs] and the expected 1-based index. The `error` scenarios pin spec-defined load-time errors and identify the diagnostic code each binding MUST raise. Bindings construct the table via {op: const, value: [...]} in the .esm; this expected.json carries the same arrays so the harness can drive the function directly without re-parsing.

scenarios.name: exact_on_boundary
scenarios.description: x = 2.0 lands exactly on xs[2]. Spec left-bias returns 2.

scenarios.name: between_entries
scenarios.description: x = 2.5 falls strictly between xs[2] and xs[3]; smallest i with xs[i] ≥ 2.5 is 3.

scenarios.name: below_minimum
scenarios.description: x < xs[1] → return 1 (clamp position at the lower end).

scenarios.name: exact_at_first
scenarios.description: x = xs[1] returns 1 (left-bias on duplicates / exact matches).

scenarios.name: above_maximum
scenarios.description: x > xs[N] → return N+1 (one past the end). For this 5-element table, expected = 6.

scenarios.name: exact_at_last
scenarios.description: x = xs[N] returns N (still satisfies xs[i] ≥ x with smallest i).

scenarios.name: nan_x
scenarios.description: NaN x is treated as 'greater than every finite element' → return N+1 = 6.

scenarios.name: single_element_below
scenarios.description: Single-element table; x below the entry returns 1.

scenarios.name: single_element_at
scenarios.description: Single-element table; x equal to the entry returns 1.

scenarios.name: single_element_above
scenarios.description: Single-element table; x above the entry returns N+1 = 2.

scenarios.name: duplicates_left_bias
scenarios.description: Duplicates in xs: returned index is the smallest i with xs[i] ≥ x. For x = 2.0 and xs = [1, 2, 2, 2, 3], expected = 2.

scenarios.name: duplicates_below_run
scenarios.description: Duplicates: x just below the run still returns the start of the run.

scenarios.name: non_decreasing_allowed
scenarios.description: Spec says 'non-decreasing' (not strictly increasing); equal-adjacent xs are valid. With xs = [1, 1, 2, 2, 3], x = 1.5 returns the smallest i with xs[i] ≥ 1.5 = 3.

error_scenarios.name: non_monotonic_xs
error_scenarios.description: xs is not non-decreasing — bindings MUST reject at the call (or load) site with diagnostic code 'searchsorted_non_monotonic'.

error_scenarios.name: nan_in_xs
error_scenarios.description: xs contains a NaN entry — bindings MUST reject with diagnostic code 'searchsorted_nan_in_table'.
